# Research - 2026-06-07

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins, agents, permissions)

> 24-hour delta on top of 2026-06-06. **Mostly hold-the-line cycle, with
> 1 close.** Upstream: claude-code v2.1.167+v2.1.168 ("Bug fixes and
> reliability improvements" only - no new features); cline v3.88.1
> (#11293 inline ask-question scroll, #11325 connector routing + stale
> hub session, #11333 vscode packaging); kilocode v7.3.39-v7.3.40
> (#10925 `:skill`/`:mcp` autocomplete suffixes, plus Bun/morphsdk
> fixes already triaged N/A). aider + continuedev produced no
> functional commits. **06-06 Item #5 (deny-rule glob warnings +
> allow-rule asymmetric guard) shipped** in commit `35bb61bc` (PR #718,
> closes issue #713). **Issue #723** filed for 06-06 Item #6 (Stop hook
> `additionalContext`). Items #1-#4, #7-#9 from 06-06 remain unfiled
> and open in code (verified: `src/utils/errors.ts` absent, `examples/`
> absent, `fallbackModel: string` still single, `additionalContext`
> not in `src/hooks/`, `browser.ts:232,276` still uses
> `err.name === 'TimeoutError'`).

## Executive Summary

- **claude-code v2.1.167-v2.1.168**: no actionable surface; both are
  detail-free "Bug fixes and reliability improvements" entries. Re-evaluate
  next cycle.
- **cline #11325 reinforces 06-06 Item #9 (stale-session recovery)**: this
  is the SECOND in-window precedent (#11259 was the first). cline names
  the error string `session not found: <id>` - adopt the same wording in
  our log output for grep-ability when we ship.
- **cline #11293 -> new Item #1 (TUI question-prompt audit, ~2h)**: cline
  fixed under-allocated vertical space in 3-option ask-question prompts
  with wrapped option text. We use Ink (not OpenTUI), but Ink also uses
  Yoga - defensive audit + Ink regression test on
  `src/cli/tui/components/MessageBubble.tsx` rendering of `question`-tool
  prompts. Likely no behaviour change; produces a regression guard.
- **kilocode #10925 -> new Item #2 (`:skill`/`:mcp` slash disambiguation,
  ~5h)**: kilocode shipped source-suffix autocomplete: a skill named
  `commit` colliding with built-in/MCP shows three entries (`/commit`,
  `/commit:skill`, `/commit:mcp`) and the suffixed form resolves
  unambiguously. Two cycles ago we shipped `/plugins` and `/skills`; we
  now have collision potential and no disambiguation
  (`grep ':skill\|:mcp' src/cli/interactive.ts src/cli/tui/ src/skill/`
  is empty). Builds on existing `mcp__server__tool` namespacing.
- **Backlog signal**: 06-06 -> 06-07 closed 1 of 9 items (#5 - 11%) and
  filed 1 of 9 as an issue (#6 - 11%). Items #1 (shell-startup
  protection) and #3 (typed `TimeoutError`) are now in their FOURTH
  cycle open; #4 (`examples/plugins/`) third; #2 (multi-model
  `fallbackModel` chain) second. Close-rate doubled vs the 0% I
  initially read - but at 22% (close + file) the planning-agent queue
  is still the main bottleneck.

## Detailed Findings

### Closed since 2026-06-06 (verified in code)

- **#5** deny-rule glob warnings + asymmetric allow-rule guard -
  shipped in commit `35bb61bc` (PR #718, closes issue #713) on
  2026-06-06 10:53. Implementation: startup `logger.warn` for
  literal-name typos in deny rules, schema-level rejection of `*`/`?`
  in allow rules unless `mcp__`-prefixed, plus
  `src/permission/__tests__/wildcard-tools.test.ts` covering all four
  cases.

### Verified-still-open from 2026-06-06 (no code change in 24h)

- **#1** shell-startup + build-tool config protection - 4th cycle open.
- **#2** multi-model `fallbackModel` chain - 2nd cycle open.
- **#3** typed `TimeoutError` (+ `AbortError`) - 4th cycle open.
- **#4** `examples/plugins/env-blocker/` + `tool-logger/` - 3rd cycle.
- **#7, #8, #9** (plugin-bundled skills, `alexi plugin uninstall`,
  stale-session recovery) - all 2nd cycle. **#9 reinforced by cline
  #11325 (second precedent)**.
- **#6** Stop hook `additionalContext` - filed as issue #723.

### New gaps this cycle

#### 1. TUI question-prompt rendering audit (Category: tool - small, ~2h)

- **What**: Audit `question`-tool render path
  (`src/tool/tools/question.ts` -> `src/cli/tui/components/ToolCallBlock.tsx`
  -> `MessageBubble.tsx`) for fixed `height={N}` on the parent box that
  would clip when a 3-option prompt has one wrapped option at narrow
  widths. Replace with `minHeight` + `flexShrink={0}` on option rows.
  Add `src/cli/tui/__tests__/question-render.test.tsx` using
  `ink-testing-library`: render 3 options at width=60 with one wrap,
  assert all three labels appear in the rendered frames.
- **Why**: cline #11293 found this exact bug class (under-allocated
  parent + wrapped child = silent visual corruption). Ink uses Yoga
  layout under the hood, same family of failure modes. Defensive audit;
  likely no behaviour change, but produces a regression test.
- **Source**: cline/cline#11293 (v3.88.1).
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: small (~2h).

#### 2. `:skill` / `:mcp` slash-command source disambiguation (Category: command - small/medium, ~5h)

- **What**: Build a unified slash-command registry mapping `/<name>` to
  `{ source: 'builtin' | 'skill' | 'mcp', resolver }` entries. When
  multiple sources contribute the same name, autocomplete shows one
  entry per source: `/commit` (builtin), `/commit:skill`, `/commit:mcp`.
  Suffixed form bypasses precedence (default: builtin > mcp > skill).
  Tests at `src/cli/__tests__/slash-autocomplete-collisions.test.ts`
  cover (a) no collision -> unsuffixed, (b) builtin+skill collision ->
  three entries, (c) suffixed form resolves to named source.
- **Why**: We have NO disambiguation (verified by grep). Two cycles
  ago we shipped `/plugins` and `/skills`, opening the door to
  user-defined skill slash commands; collisions today silently shadow.
  Convention parallels existing `mcp__server__tool` tool namespacing.
- **Source**: Kilo-Org/kilocode#10925 (v7.3.39).
- **Category**: command. **Size**: small/medium (~5h).
- **Pre-flight**: verify a skill-as-slash bridge exists today. If not,
  ship the bridge in the same PR or a precursor; otherwise this finding
  has no collisions to disambiguate.

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Planning-agent priority: file the 06-06 backlog as
   `auto-implement` issues** (7 of 9 still unfiled, after #5 closed and
   #6 filed). Order by leverage: 06-06 #1 (shell-startup, 4th cycle),
   #3 (`TimeoutError`, 4th cycle), #2 (multi-model fallback, marquee),
   #4 (`examples/plugins/`), then #7/#8/#9.
2. **This cycle's small additions** are independent: new Item #1
   (~2h, defensive audit) and new Item #2 (~5h, slash disambiguation).
   Each ships as its own PR.
3. **Item #9 (stale-session recovery) is now twice-precedented** (cline
   #11259 + #11325). When filed, link both PRs and adopt the
   `session not found: <id>` error-string format.
4. **Reference-repo rotation**: continuedev has shipped no functional
   commits since 2026-04-17. If the dry-spell extends one more cycle,
   substitute `sst/opencode` or `opencode-ai/opencode` from the role
   file's reference list.
5. **Backlog hygiene**: 06-02 -> 06-06 closed 40% (2/5); 06-06 -> 06-07
   closed 11% (1/9) + filed 11% (1/9) = 22% throughput. Net backlog
   still grows because the cycle added 2 new items; surface in the
   planning doc as a planning-agent throughput signal, not a
   research-agent one.
